context: Tests whether the agent uses the level:metric:operations naming convention for recording rules and correctly identifies and remedies the high-cardinality label anti-pattern in the sum by (user_id) query.

checklist.name: Recording rule naming convention
checklist.description: All three recording rules use the level:metric:operations naming format (e.g., job:http_requests:rate5m) — NOT arbitrary names

checklist.name: Three recording rules present
checklist.description: recording_rules.yaml contains exactly three 'record:' entries covering request rate, error rate, and latency

checklist.name: Valid YAML recording rule format
checklist.description: The file uses valid Prometheus recording rule YAML with 'record:' and 'expr:' keys (and optionally 'labels:')

checklist.name: High-cardinality label identified
checklist.description: cardinality_analysis.md explicitly identifies user_id as a high-cardinality label and explains it produces too many series

checklist.name: Safer alternative query provided
checklist.description: cardinality_analysis.md provides a safer alternative query that does NOT group by user_id (e.g., groups by job, status_code, or other low-cardinality labels)

checklist.name: by() on all recording rule exprs
checklist.description: All three recording rule expressions include an explicit by() clause in any aggregation

checklist.name: Latency rule uses histogram_quantile
checklist.description: The latency recording rule expression uses histogram_quantile(0.95, ...) with sum by (job, le)

checklist.name: Label filter in error rate rule
checklist.description: The error rate recording rule expression includes a status_code label filter to select 5xx errors
